description_long: This is a hand axe featuring a straight, cylindrical wooden handle with a natural wood grain finish. The handle is attached to a single, broad, wedge-shaped metal blade that is dark gray near the mounting and transitions to a lighter metallic edge. The blade has a slightly curved cutting edge and a flat poll opposite the edge, suitable for chopping or splitting tasks. The mounting of the blade appears traditional, with the head fitted securely onto the handle. There are no visible decorative elements, engravings, or branding on either the handle or the blade. The overall design is utilitarian, typical of axes used for wood chopping or general-purpose tasks in urban or suburban environments.
description: A utilitarian hand axe with a straight wooden handle and a broad, curved metal blade, designed for chopping or splitting tasks. The blade is securely mounted and lacks visible markings or decorative features.
description_view_0: The axe is shown at an angle, highlighting the full length of the wooden handle and the broad, curved blade with its metallic edge.
description_view_1: The axe is viewed from the top, showing the circular cross-section of the handle and the thickness of the blade's mounting.
description_view_2: The axe is displayed from the opposite side, clearly showing the straight handle and the full profile of the blade's cutting edge.
description_view_3: The axe is seen from the bottom, emphasizing the length of the handle and the attachment point of the blade.
attribute_handle_type: Straight cylindrical wooden handle with a smooth finish and round cross-section, no visible grip features.
attribute_blade_design: Single broad wedge-shaped steel blade with a curved cutting edge and flat poll, no beard or decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the blade fitted onto the handle.
